144 - How To Frame a Sales Meeting to your Advantage - YouTube.mp4
Now in this video, you're going to learn how to effectively start a meeting or a presentation to automatically draw on your prospects' interests to what you're offering, instead of going into your sales pitch, which as we know, triggers sales pressure. Question: How do you think your potential customers feel when you're about to launch into your sales pitch and do a presentation? Do you think that they're going to be excited about all the wonderful features and benefits that you're there to tell them about? Or do you think they're going to become tense and put up their defensive walls? What usually happens? We know. Do you find that many potential customers move away from you when you start to present? Your potential customers, more than likely, will always move away from you as you start to present because it always triggers sales pressure.
Why? You see, because most of your prospects, when you're presenting them, fear that you're going to try to sell them something. So with this going on in their mind the whole time that you're presenting, do you think they're really listening to you? Very unlikely. You see, one of the biggest reasons why you might be getting average results in selling is because you present or you propose your solutions way too early before ever even discovering the deeper needs of your potential customer. Now when you do this, your prospects will always look at you as just another salesperson trying to sell them something. You're no different than anyone else. They don't trust you, so they don't buy from you. Remember, when you present without having any knowledge of that person, their company, it's only your interpretation of what you think the other person needs to know.
I mean it literally is like taking a bucket of mud and throwing it up against the wall hoping some of it sticks. What if, instead of hope, hoping, what mud would stick, you actually knew which mud would stick? How many times have you done a presentation based on nothing but your assumption? And it ends up with your potential customer saying, 'That's not for us,' or 'Let me think it over,' or 'Why don't you call me back in a few months?' How does that make you feel when that happens? Today's customer doesn't want to be talked to and sold. They want to be asked and heard— whether it's spoken out loud or not. The question that most of your prospects have when they're expecting a presentation is, 'What have you got or 'What do you want to tell me about your product or service or 'What are you trying to sell me Now, the average salesperson's first inclination is to do what?
It's to tell them. Don't do it unless you want to keep getting average results. Whether you're asked or not, start off by asking this. I want to welcome all of you to the meeting today. And before I go into who we are and what we do and all that kind of boring stuff, it might be appropriate if we asked you a few questions. To find out a little bit more about you and what you're possibly looking for, to see if we could actually help you. For example, who do you use for your telephone services now? Let's say, if you sold telephone services. Now, here are a few more starter phrases for a meeting that you can use.
Before I get into what we do, it might be helpful to the both of us if I could understand a little bit more about you and what you're looking for. For example, who do you use for your lead generation now? Let's say, if you sold leads. Here's another starter phrase. Mr. Jones, before I get into what we do, perhaps you would give me just your thoughts on what we spoke about previously. Let's say that was the second meeting. Here's another one. Mary, before I go into what we do, with your permission, I'd like to ask you a few questions about you and possibly what you're looking for to see if we can even help you. Here's another starter phrase. Jane, for example, can you tell me more about the company that you're using now to find better employees?
That's if you sold staffing services. You would use that starter phrase. This is what a seven-figure salesperson asks. Now, I want to thank you for watching this video. About how to effectively start a meeting and a presentation to automatically draw in your prospects to what you're offering instead of going into your sales pitch, which automatically triggers sales pressure from the very beginning. Now in the next video, we're going to discuss how to call referrals and how to have them instantly connect with you.
